expectations.text: Identifies the issue as decision fatigue caused by unlimited choices, not a lack of design skill
expectations.evidence: 'The paralysis you're feeling is a systems problem, not a taste problem. When you pick a font size or color from an unlimited pool of possible values, of course you'll second-guess yourself — the design space is infinite'

expectations.text: Recommends defining design systems upfront (type scale, color palette, spacing scale)
expectations.evidence: Table: 'Systems to define before you start designing: Font sizes (12,14,16,18,20,24,...), Font weights (400,500,600,700), Spacing/padding (4,8,12,16,...), Border radii, Shadows'

expectations.text: Explains how constrained options make decisions easier — process of elimination from a small set
expectations.evidence: 'You're not searching infinite space anymore. You're doing binary elimination between 2–3 options. This is fast and produces confident decisions' — exact mechanism described

eval_feedback.overall: All three pass. The skill adds unique value: the grayscale-first principle, personality framework, and feature-first approach — none are covered by the assertions but represent genuine skill differentiation.
